metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Which day is observed as Goods and Services Tax (GST) Day every year?
questions.explanation: Recently, India celebrated GST Day on July 1 to mark eight years since the Goods and Services Tax was implemented in 2017. Goods and Services Tax transformed the indirect tax system by making compliance easier, reducing business costs, and enabling free movement of goods across states. It boosted transparency and efficiency, supporting a stronger and more unified economy. In 2024–2025, gross Goods and Services Tax collections reached a record ₹22.08 lakh crore. This shows a 9.4 percent yearly growth, reflecting better tax compliance and higher formalisation of the economy.

questions.question: World Zoonoses Day is observed annually on which day to raise awareness about zoonotic diseases?
questions.explanation: World Zoonoses Day is observed every year on July 6th to raise awareness about zoonotic diseases. In India, the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) highlighted the need to fast-track efforts to eliminate dog-mediated rabies by 2030. Around 9.1 million animal bites happen in India every year. The study used a decision tree model to estimate animal bites and rabies deaths.

questions.question: Income Tax Day is celebrated on which day every year in India?
questions.explanation: India celebrates Income Tax Day on 24th July 2025, marking the 166th anniversary of the introduction of income tax in 1860 by Sir James Wilson to manage post-1857 war expenses. This day honours the evolution of India’s tax system and its role in nation-building. The tax system progressed from the Income Tax Act of 1922 to the comprehensive Income Tax Act of 1961. Major reforms began with Permanent Account Number (PAN) in 1972, followed by computerization in 1981 and the new PAN series in 1995. The Income Tax Bill 2025 has been tabled to replace the 1961 Act, aiming to simplify language, remove outdated clauses, and enhance clarity.

questions.question: World Drowning Prevention Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Drowning Prevention Day is observed every year on 25 July to spread awareness on drowning as a preventable public health issue. It is recognised by the United Nations (UN) and focuses on saving lives, especially in low- and middle-income countries. Around 2.36 lakh people die from drowning each year, with children, adolescents, and the poor being the most affected.

questions.question: Kargil Vijay Diwas is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: Recently, India observed the 26th Kargil Vijay Diwas on 26 July 2025. It marks India’s victory in the 1999 Kargil War against Pakistani intruders. Brave soldiers reclaimed high-altitude posts under Operation Vijay. The enemy had occupied peaks along the Line of Control to cut off National Highway 1A. Indian troops fought in harsh terrain and weather to regain every inch. The day honours the valour, unity, and sacrifice of our soldiers. It reminds us to safeguard India’s sovereignty with courage and resolve.

questions.question: International Tiger Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: International Tiger Day is observed every year on July 29 to raise awareness about tiger conservation. The day was first established in 2010 during the Saint Petersburg Tiger Summit. Thirteen tiger-range countries participated in the summit, including India, Bangladesh, Nepal, Bhutan, Malaysia, and Russia. The summit launched the “Tx2” program, aiming to double the global tiger population by 2022. World Wildlife Fund (WWF) reports that tiger numbers have dropped from 100,000 a century ago to around 4,000 today. The day highlights the urgent need to protect tiger habitats and support conservation globally.

questions.question: Which state government has declared August 7 as ‘Sustainable Agriculture Day’ in honour of Dr. M. S. Swaminathan?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Maharashtra government declared August 7 will be celebrated every year as “Sustainable Agriculture Day” to honour Bharat Ratna Dr. M. S. Swaminathan. The announcement marks the birth centenary of Dr. Swaminathan, known as the architect of India’s Green Revolution. Dr. Swaminathan’s research boosted wheat and rice productivity, helping India achieve food self-sufficiency and economic growth. He was awarded the Bharat Ratna by the Government of India in 2024. The United Nations recognised him as the father of the economic environment.

questions.question: World Sanskrit Day 2025 was celebrated on which day?
questions.explanation: World Sanskrit Day 2025 was celebrated on August 9, coinciding with Shravan Poornima and Raksha Bandhan. The day honoured Sanskrit, also called Devavani or “language of the gods,” one of the world’s oldest and most structured languages. Sanskrit had been the medium for India’s ancient scriptures, philosophies, and scientific works for thousands of years. The day was initiated by the Government of India in 1969. The main purpose was to promote and revive the Sanskrit language.

questions.question: World Elephant Day is celebrated every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Elephant Day is celebrated every year on 12th August to raise global awareness for elephant conservation and protect their habitats. On 12th August 2025, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC) and Tamil Nadu Forest Department celebrated World Elephant Day in Coimbatore. A workshop on Human-Elephant Conflict (HEC) was held for elephant range states to share challenges and mitigation strategies under Project Elephant. World Elephant Day was co-founded on 12th August 2012 by Patricia Sims and the Elephant Reintroduction Foundation of Thailand.

questions.question: Which organization hosted ‘Mega Tinkering Day 2025’, India’s largest school-based tinkering event?
questions.explanation: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM), NITI Aayog, hosted ‘Mega Tinkering Day 2025’, India’s largest school-based tinkering event. More than 10,000 Atal Tinkering Labs (ATLs) from all 35 states and union territories participated virtually. The activity was guided online with real-time collaboration, involving students from remote areas like Leh, Kargil, Virudhunagar, Manipur, Mizoram, Arunachal Pradesh, Kanniyakumari, Bhuj, and Kutchh. The initiative showcased grassroots innovation, aligning with the Prime Minister’s vision of “Viksit Bharat” and making India a global leader in school-level innovation.
